function.name: analyze_bug
function.description: Analyse a bug report, stack trace, or failing test to identify the root cause and propose targeted fixes. Produces a ranked list of hypotheses with supporting evidence and recommended remediation steps.

description.description: Human-readable description of the observed bug or unexpected behaviour.

stack_trace.description: Full stack trace or error message from the failing execution, if available.

code_snippet.description: Relevant source code excerpt where the bug likely originates.

language.description: Programming language of the affected code.

steps_to_reproduce.description: Ordered list of steps that reliably reproduce the bug.

environment.description: Runtime environment details (OS, runtime version, dependency versions, etc.).

root_cause.description: Most likely root cause of the bug.

recommended_tests.description: Test scenarios to add to prevent regression.

examples.description: Analyse a null-pointer bug in a Python service

input.description: Service crashes when a user has no email address set.

output.root_cause: user.email is None for users who registered without providing an email address. The code does not guard against this before calling .lower().

hypotheses.hypothesis: email field is optional in the data model but treated as required in normalize_email()
hypotheses.evidence: Stack trace points to line 42 where .lower() is called without a None check.

fix_suggestions.description: Add a None guard before calling .lower()
fix_suggestions.code_patch: return user.email.lower() if user.email else None

function.name: check_coverage
function.description: Analyse existing tests against source code to identify coverage gaps, untested branches, and missing edge cases. Returns a prioritised list of gaps with suggested test cases to fill them.

source_code.description: Source code (function, module, or file) to check coverage for.

existing_tests.description: Existing test code for the source. Can be the full test file or just a list of test names.

language.description: Programming language of the source code.

coverage_report.description: Optional coverage report output (e.g. from Istanbul, pytest-cov, JaCoCo) to supplement static analysis.

meets_minimum.description: Whether estimated coverage meets the minimum_coverage threshold.

examples.description: Check coverage for a payment processing module

gaps.description: Expired card path (card.is_expired() == True) is not tested.
Gateway failure path (result.success == False) is not tested.

output.summary: Current coverage is ~60%, below the 90% target. Two high-priority branches are untested.

function.name: create_test_plan
function.description: Generate a comprehensive test plan for a feature, user story, or requirement. Produces a structured document covering test objectives, scope, test types, entry/exit criteria, and individual test cases with priority levels.

feature.description: Name or title of the feature or user story to test.

requirements.description: Feature requirements, acceptance criteria, or user story description.

test_types.description: Types of testing to include in the plan.

risk_areas.description: Known risk areas or edge cases that deserve extra attention.

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly excluded areas or test types.

environment.description: Target environment or environments for testing (e.g. 'staging', 'production-like').

examples.description: Create a test plan for a user login feature

input.requirements: Users can log in with email and password. After 5 failed attempts the account is locked for 15 minutes. Passwords are never stored in plain text.

output.objective: Verify that the User Login feature works correctly under normal and adversarial conditions, including account lockout and secure password handling.
entry_criteria: Auth service is deployed to staging
exit_criteria: All critical and high-priority test cases pass

test_cases.title: Successful login with valid credentials
preconditions: User account exists with known email and password
steps: POST /auth/login with valid email and password
test_cases.expected_result: 200 OK with a valid session token

risks: Lockout timer may not be consistent under high load

function.name: generate_tests
function.description: Generate unit, integration, or end-to-end tests for a given code snippet or function. Infers the testing framework from the language and project context when not specified. Returns ready-to-run test code with descriptive test names and inline comments.

code.description: The source code (function, class, or module) to generate tests for.

language.description: Programming language of the source code (e.g. 'python', 'typescript', 'java', 'go').

test_type.description: The kind of tests to generate. Defaults to 'unit'.

framework.description: Testing framework to use (e.g. 'pytest', 'jest', 'junit', 'go test'). Inferred from language when omitted.

coverage_targets.description: Specific scenarios or edge cases that must be covered (e.g. 'empty input', 'null values', 'concurrent access').

include_mocks.description: Whether to generate mocks or stubs for external dependencies.

returns.description: Generated test code along with metadata about what was produced.

test_code.description: The complete, runnable test file content.

test_cases.description: List of test case names that were generated.

notes.description: Any caveats, assumptions, or setup instructions.

examples.description: Generate pytest unit tests for a Python function

output.notes: Ensure the module containing `divide` is importable from the test file.

function.name: review_code
function.description: Perform a thorough QA-focused code review of a given snippet or file. Identifies bugs, edge-case failures, missing error handling, security issues, and deviations from best practices. Returns structured findings with severity levels and actionable fix suggestions.

focus_areas.description: Specific categories to prioritise during the review. Defaults to all areas.

context.description: Optional context about the code's purpose, upstream callers, or performance requirements.

standards.description: Coding standards or style guides to check against (e.g. 'PEP 8', 'Google Java Style', 'OWASP Top 10').

examples.description: Review a TypeScript function for common issues

output.summary: The function lacks error handling for failed HTTP requests and does not validate the 'id' parameter before interpolation.

findings.description: fetch() can reject or return a non-2xx status without throwing. Neither case is handled.
findings.suggestion: Check res.ok and wrap in try/catch: if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`).

findings.description: The 'id' parameter is interpolated directly into the URL without validation.
findings.suggestion: Validate 'id' against an expected format (e.g. UUID) before use.

function.name: validate_requirements
function.description: Evaluate a set of requirements or acceptance criteria for testability, completeness, and ambiguity. Returns a structured report highlighting unclear, missing, or conflicting requirements along with recommendations to make them testable.

requirements.description: Requirements, acceptance criteria, or user stories to validate (plain text or Gherkin/BDD format).

context.description: Optional product or domain context that helps interpret the requirements.

checks.description: Specific quality checks to perform. Defaults to all.

testable_criteria.description: Rewritten, testable versions of the requirements.

input.requirements: The system should be fast and handle many users at the same time.

issues.description: 'Fast' is not measurable. No latency or throughput target is defined.
issues.recommendation: Specify a concrete metric, e.g. 'p99 latency < 200ms under 500 concurrent users'.

issues.description: 'Many users' is undefined and cannot be validated by a test.
issues.recommendation: Define a concrete concurrency target, e.g. '1,000 concurrent users'.

testable_criteria: The API must respond within 200ms at the p99 percentile under a load of 500 concurrent users.
The system must sustain 1,000 concurrent active sessions without error rates exceeding 0.1%.
